pulse.summary: Developer copilots are hot, but the mobile-specific angle is less saturated than the general coding market.
pulse.whitespace: Focus on mobile release workflows, debugging on-device issues, and app store readiness.
top_signals: Mobile teams still wrestle with platform-specific friction.
Shipping workflows can be more defensible than raw code completion.

nodes.label: AI mobile coding copilot
nodes.summary: An assistant for building and shipping mobile apps faster.

nodes.label: GitHub Copilot
nodes.summary: General coding copilot with broad IDE coverage.

nodes.label: Cursor
nodes.summary: AI-first coding editor for software teams.

copilot.name: GitHub Copilot
copilot.why_it_wins: Distribution and familiarity make it the baseline for many engineering teams.
risks: Not tailored to mobile release pain.

cursor.name: Cursor
cursor.why_it_wins: Strong workflow integration for teams that want the editor itself to be AI-native.

replit.name: Replit
replit.why_it_wins: Combines coding, execution, and sharing in one environment.
risks: Mobile-native teams may still prefer their own toolchains.

pulse.summary: The general coding copilot market is brutally competitive, but mobile teams still have distinct release, debugging, and compliance pain that broad products only partially address.
pulse.whitespace: Specialize on the last mile of mobile shipping: builds, testing, crashes, store metadata, and release confidence.
top_signals: Mobile platform quirks still slow teams down.
Teams pay for anything that reduces release risk.

nodes.label: AI mobile coding copilot
nodes.summary: An assistant for building and shipping mobile apps faster.

nodes.label: GitHub Copilot
nodes.summary: General coding copilot with broad IDE coverage.

nodes.label: Cursor
nodes.summary: AI-first coding editor with deep workflow integration.

nodes.label: Replit
nodes.summary: Agent-driven coding with execution and sharing.

nodes.label: Codeium
nodes.summary: Autocomplete and AI coding support across editors.

nodes.label: Bolt.new
nodes.summary: Prompt-to-app experience for fast product prototyping.

nodes.label: Developer copilots
nodes.summary: General AI tooling for code writing and editing.

nodes.label: Mobile release ops
nodes.summary: Build pipelines, testing, crash triage, and store submission workflows.

nodes.label: Release confidence copilot
nodes.summary: An assistant tuned for test failures, app store requirements, and mobile regressions.

copilot.name: GitHub Copilot
copilot.why_it_wins: It sets the default expectation for AI assistance inside familiar developer workflows.
risks: Broad positioning makes mobile-specific depth harder.
Competes on platform reach rather than niche focus.

cursor.name: Cursor
cursor.why_it_wins: Strong editing loop and agent-like workflow make it a serious option for high-intensity engineers.
risks: Mobile-specific ops remain outside the editor.

replit.name: Replit
replit.why_it_wins: Combines generation, execution, and collaboration in one loop.
risks: Serious mobile teams still rely on native tooling.

codeium.name: Codeium
codeium.why_it_wins: Provides a credible lower-friction alternative in the baseline copilot category.
risks: Hard to stand out if the product stays generic.

bolt.name: Bolt.new
bolt.why_it_wins: Delivers very fast visible progress, which is compelling for MVP builders.
risks: Prototype focus is not the same as production mobile shipping.

brutal_truth.title: Brutal Truth
brutal_truth.headline: You cannot win by being another general coding copilot.
brutal_truth.body: The broad market already belongs to products with enormous distribution, editor lock-in, or platform depth. A mobile-focused product needs to attach itself to pain they do not fully solve.
bullets: Code completion alone is not enough.
Developer trust comes from reducing release risk, not flashy demos.
Platform-specific pain is where the wedge lives.

opportunity.title: Opportunity
opportunity.headline: Become the release confidence layer for mobile teams.
opportunity.body: Mobile engineers still lose time to flaky builds, crash triage, store metadata, screenshots, and platform review friction. A copilot that owns this last-mile workflow can be more specific, more valuable, and more defensible.
bullets: Start with React Native or Flutter teams.
Own test failure diagnosis and app store readiness.
Tie pricing to release throughput or regression reduction.
